Score technical accuracy on a scale of 0-10.

Technical accuracy criteria: Are all Git concepts described correctly? Are branch names and workflow steps precise? Are all claims about GitFlow and trunk-based development factually accurate?

If the technical accuracy score is below 8.0, identify every inaccurate or imprecise claim, correct each one individually, and re-score the revised article. Continue this loop until the technical accuracy score reaches 8.0 or above before proceeding.
